title: Build Like a Pro
tagline: Professional structure and your first portfolio.

lessons.title: Build Pages Like a Professional (Semantic HTML)

accomplishments: Transform a basic page into a professionally structured page
Understand WHY structure matters for accessibility
Make your code readable and SEO-friendly

do.title: Transform divs with semantic tags
steps: Notice: it looks the same but the code has meaning now
do.code:
Sarah's Coding Journey

Learning one step at a time

  • Home
  • About
  • Blog

My First Week of HTML

I thought I'd never understand tags...

© 2024 Sarah Martinez | Built with HTML
do.result: Now the code has semantic meaning. Screen readers can understand your structure!
do.proofRequired: your code showing the semantic tags (<header>, <nav>, <main>, <footer>)

concepts.name: <header> tag
concepts.definition: Top of page or section. Contains site title, logo, tagline, or article metadata (title, author, date).
concepts.analogy: The letterhead on official paper - identifies what document this is.

concepts.name: <nav> tag
concepts.definition: Navigation links. Screen readers can jump straight to navigation with one keystroke!
concepts.analogy: The table of contents in a book - helps you find different sections.

concepts.name: <main> tag
concepts.definition: Primary content. Only ONE per page. This is THE content people came for. Screen readers can skip straight here!
concepts.analogy: The article in a magazine - not the ads, not the index, just the story.

concepts.name: <article> tag
concepts.definition: Self-contained content. Test: Could this stand alone if copied to another site? If YES → use <article>.
concepts.analogy: A newspaper article you could cut out and share independently.

concepts.name: <section> tag
concepts.definition: Thematic grouping of content. Each section should have a heading.
concepts.analogy: Chapters in a book - different themes, different sections.

concepts.name: <aside> tag
concepts.definition: Related but not main content. Sidebars, pull quotes, related links, fun fact boxes.
concepts.analogy: Sidebar notes in a textbook - related info but not the main lesson.

concepts.name: <footer> tag
concepts.definition: Bottom of page or section. Copyright info, contact details, social links, site map.
concepts.analogy: The fine print at the bottom of a contract.

understand.keyTakeaway: Use the RIGHT tag for the job. Semantic HTML makes your code accessible to screen readers and understandable to search engines.

build.goal: Build a complete semantic page using ALL the semantic tags

codeComparison.old:
Site Title
Links
Main content
Copyright

build.hint: You can have multiple headers and footers (page header, article header, etc.) but only ONE <main> per page

challenge.title: Restructure Your Portfolio Site
challenge.mission: Go back to your existing pages and replace all divs with proper semantic tags.
requirements: Wrap your navigation in <nav> with a list
If you have blog posts or projects, use <article>
Ensure heading hierarchy is correct (h1 → h2 → h3, no skipping)
challenge.starterCode:
Your Name

Your tagline

  • Home

© 2024 Your Name
challenge.bonusChallenge: Add <time> tags around dates with proper 'datetime' attributes, and use <mark> to highlight important text

capabilities: Use <header> for page and section headers
Understand why semantic HTML matters for accessibility
Write code that screen readers can understand

bridge.preview: In Lesson 8, you'll learn text formatting (<strong>, <em>, <mark>), special characters, and developer tools for debugging your code.

lessons.title: Build a Real Portfolio Website You Can Share

accomplishments: Create a multi-page portfolio website with everything you've learned
Add images, lists, forms, and formatted text
Deploy it live on the internet (free!)

do.title: Set up your project and build the home page
steps: Create a new folder on your Desktop called my-portfolio
Inside that folder, create these files: index.html, about.html, projects.html, contact.html
Create a subfolder called images inside my-portfolio
Copy the starter code below into index.html
Replace [Your Name] with your actual name
Save and open index.html in your browser
do.code:
Home About Projects Contact

Welcome! I'm [Your Name]

I'm learning to build websites with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

What I'm Learning

  • HTML structure and semantic elements
  • CSS styling and layouts
  • Building interactive features

Recent Work

Check out my projects page to see what I've built!
do.result: You built a professional home page with navigation!
do.proofRequired: Screenshot of your home page in the browser with your name.

concepts.name: Multi-page structure
concepts.definition: Real websites have multiple HTML files linked together through navigation. Each page is its own .html file.
concepts.analogy: Rooms in a house connected by hallways (navigation links).

concepts.name: Consistent navigation
concepts.definition: The same nav bar appears on every page so users can always get around. Copy it to each file.
concepts.analogy: The menu at a restaurant — same options no matter what page you're on.

concepts.name: Deployment
concepts.definition: Putting your files on a server so anyone with the URL can see your site. Netlify does this for free by drag-and-drop.
concepts.analogy: Moving your art from your studio to a gallery where everyone can see it.

understand.keyTakeaway: A portfolio is just multiple HTML files with the same navigation, organized in one folder, uploaded to a hosting service.

build.goal: Build the About, Projects, and Contact pages using the same nav bar and style

codeComparison.new:
<!-- Complete portfolio -->
my-portfolio/
├── index.html      (Home)
├── about.html      (Your story + skills)
├── projects.html   (What you've built)
├── contact.html    (Form to reach you)
└── images/         (Photos + screenshots)

build.hint: Copy the entire <style> and <nav> sections from index.html into each new page to keep everything consistent.

challenge.title: Make It Yours & Deploy It Live
challenge.mission: Customize your portfolio with your own colors, photos, and content — then deploy it to the internet for free!
requirements: Change colors to your own color scheme
Add a profile photo to the About page
Add screenshots of your projects to the Projects page
Write unique content — make it personal!
Add a footer with social links on every page
Deploy to Netlify: go to netlify.com, sign up free, drag your folder

capabilities: Build a complete multi-page website from scratch
Organize files in a professional project structure
